response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior generalist with deep web, backend, and testing expertise, but does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth, and no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was a retail investment fintech, not capital markets systems. The gap on all three mandatory pillars makes this a poor fit despite otherwise solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and test tooling authorship
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and none on Windows-focused financial systems
No Fixed Income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is mostly MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. 5–10 year mandate
No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
